color_profiles.name: spinach-jade
color_profiles.cause: Moderate to high Fe2+ substituting for Mg in actinolite-rich nephrite; Fe-rich composition.

color_profiles.name: mutton-fat-jade
color_profiles.cause: Low Fe content; near-pure tremolite composition; fine texture diffuses light.

color_profiles.name: celadon-jade
color_profiles.cause: Low Fe content with minor chromophores.

color_profiles.name: gray-nephrite
color_profiles.cause: Graphite inclusions or specific Fe oxidation states.

color_profiles.name: black-nephrite
color_profiles.cause: Very high Fe content and/or carbonaceous inclusions.

visual_notes: Nephrite's waxy sheen and subtle translucency are distinctive. White mutton-fat jade has a soft luminosity unlike any other stone. The green varieties can look similar to jadeite but typically show a more subdued, less intense green. The fibrous microstructure is visible under magnification as a tangled felt-like mass.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Generally inert; some material shows very weak cream or green fluorescence under LW UV
